items.item: Are cold and hot drinking water available at the washing-up area?
Is fresh water available at hose connections for washing down?
In vessel over 1000 GT, is the mechanical ventilation of the galley operational?
Is the artificial lighting of the galley operational?
Are provision store rooms being used exclusively for the intended purpose?
Are provision store rooms being maintained in a clean and efficient condition?
Is the lighting of provision store rooms operational?
Are refrigerated provisions rooms being exclusively used for the intended purpose?
Are the refrigerated spaces maintained in clean and efficient conditions?
Are adequate temperatures maintained inside the refrigerated spaces?
In walk-in rooms, can doors be opened from both sides and is the alarm operational?
Is drinking water available at galleys, main pantries and adjacent to messrooms?
Are drinking and sanitary water tanks being exclusively used for the intended purpose and
intended purpose and kept in an internally clean condition?
Are fresh water evaporators and water treatment equipment, if used to supply drinking or
sanitary fresh water, operational (including automatic control systems and alarms)?
In vessel over 1000 GT, are fresh water power pumps and reserve pumps operational?
In vessel over 1000 GT, is cool drinking water available from al least one source?
Are external bulkheads and other bulkheads required to be watertight or gastight (such as those
separating the crew accommodation from machinery spaces, storerooms, etc) maintained in an
adequate condition?
Is the insulation of external bulkheads and other bulkheads which are required to be insulated
against heat properly maintained?
Is the insulation of steam and hot water pipes crossing the crew accommodation properly
maintained so as to avoid danger and discomfort to the occupants?
If cabins and messroms are painted, is the paint used of a light colour?
Are wall surfaces, floors and ceilings well maintained, and being renewed or restored when necessary?
Are cabins, messrooms, recreations rooms if provided, and public sanitary spaces properly
ventilated, without stale air, odours or an excessive temperature in relation to the outside?
Are the ventilation systems indicated in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation Details
operational?
Are the heating system, if any, indicated in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation
Details operational?
Are radiators, if fitted, of a fixed type and adequately shielded to prevent injury to personnel
and to minimize the risk of fire?
Has the inspector verified that portable stoves are not used on board?
Are the sleeping arrangements for the crew in accordance with the Record of Approved Crew
Accommodation details and, in particular with the assignment of numbers and categories of
persons to each sleeping room?
Using the checklists provided in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation Details, have
the furnishings and particulars of each of the sleeping rooms been verified and found acceptable?
In particular, are the ventilation, heating and electric lights of each sleeping room operational?
Are messrooms and their furnishings in good state of maintenance, with numbers of seats, tables,
etc, as indicated in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation Details?
In particular, are the ventilation, heating and electric lights of each messroom in working order?
Are the open deck areas designated for recreation of the crew in good condition and free of
obstructions which may impede their use?
Is crew recreation accommodation in good condition, adequately furnished, ventilated, heated
and with electric lights as indicated in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation Details?
Are the public bathrooms in a good state of maintenance, with walls, floors and fixtures in
working order?
Are the waste pipes and soil pipes free of obstruction and in working order, without leaks which
may create health hazards?
Are cold fresh water supplies and hot fresh water supplies, or means to heat water, in working
order in all public bathrooms?
Is fresh water available in public bathrooms at all times or if not continuously in adequate
quantities and time periods?
Is flushing water available in WC's at all times and are local controls at each unit operational?
Are private and semi-private bathrooms in good state of maintenance, with fixtures, water supplies
and drains in working order?
Are WC screens, doors and, if fitted, locks in good order?
Are floor drains in bathroom used by more than one person in working order?
Are the facilities for washing clothes as indicated in the Record of Approved Crew Accommodation
Details in working order?
Are the facilities for drying clothes also in working order?
If provided, is the hospital in good state of maintenance, not used for other than its intended
purpose and with its bathroom in working order?
Is the medicine chest properly maintained, with its contents valid and renewed as necessary,
and instructions available?
If the ship is of 3000 GT or more, are the deck and engine offices well maintained and used
for the intended purpose?
If the ship regularly trades to mosquito infested areas, are the screens for side scuttles, ventilators
and doors available and in good order?
If the ship regularly trades to the Tropics or the Persian Gulf, and does not have air conditioning,
are the awnings for use in exposed deck area above the crew accommodation and open deck
recreation areas available and in good order?
Are the arrangements on board adapted, as far as is reasonable and practicable, to the particular
national customs of the crew or, when several national groups are employed, are reasonable
arrangements made to cope with these differences?
Is the crew accommodation maintained in a clean and decent condition and free of goods and
stores not the property of the crew?
Are weekly Inspections conducted by the Master, or a person delegated by the Master, accompanied
by one or more members of the crew, and are their results entered in the ship's log book?

items.item: The Cargo Gear register on board, issued by: PHRS has been examined and found up-to-date and free of any recommendations.Last Annual survey carried out on: Last Quadrennial survey carried out on :
Each part of the gear, deck machinery and its power supply has been visually inspected and found in cargo worthy condition, complete and without any visible defect.
Each crane, winch has been observed in operation and each part has been found in good working order.
The effective performance rate of loading have been checked and found satisfactory. Operating cycle time: mm, OR
No opportunity was afforded at the survey to examine the Cargo Gear in operation as shore cranes and gear were being used to load/unload the vessel’s cargo.
Hatch coamings and covers with their opening and closing appliances were examined and found satisfactory.
Companion ways leading to cargo spaces and their closing appliances were examined and found satisfactory.
Access ladders leading to cargo spaces were examined and found satisfactory.
Cargo bilge pumping arrangements were generally examined and tested under working condition and found satisfactory.
Cargo spaces ventilator coamings, heads and their closing appliances were examined and found satisfactory.
The cleanliness and general condition of the cargo holds No. , were examined and found satisfactory.
(Alternatively if B.1 to B.5 cannot be examined).

Load Line and Classification Certificate on board have been checked and the following dates found as concerns annual and periodical surveys:Last Annual Load Line survey on: ,Last annual Classification survey on: ,Last special survey on accessories (vent. coamings), bilge pump, tweendecks(if any), access ladders, working condition of closing arrangements on :

Following the above mentioned surveys and satisfactory examination of works and measures considered necessary, but subject to the condition of parts which could not be inspected. The undersigned considers that on the present date the items mentioned above are in satisfactory condition.

The term gear & tackle is defined as the standing and running signing attached to the gear in the context of the above mentioned requirements. The society understands the term “Cargo worthy” to relate to this ability of a ship’s cargo handling equipment to transfer cargo from the ship to shore and reciprocally.

Major non-conformity downgraded to minor for reason stated below:

tables.title: Equipment Table 1
rows: Corrective actions that should be completed by the Ship within the period not exceeding three (3) months from the date of issue:
Proposed Corrective action by the CSO/SSO :
If Ship does not close out the Non-Conformities and/or submit report on closing of Non-conformities timely to PhRS, will have reason to withdraw the International Ship Security Certificate.
Corrective Action Accepted(To be completed by PhRS Auditor)

items.item: General requirements
Does the company have valid state license for crewing activity by the appropriate national authority?
Does the company operate on the basis of a quality management system in line with standard ISO 9001?
Has the Crewing Company Policy been developed?
Have the aims of crewing services been defined?
MLC representative of the company is mentioned:
Requirements for crewing services
Have the requirements for organization chart, work organization and Company resources influencing on provided services been defined?
Have the requirements for professional preparation, competency and condition of crew health been defined?
Have the requirements for social protection of sailors and their families been defined?
Requirements for documentation

Does the organization chart of the Company foresee such system of responsibility, management and interrelation of personnel that provides the required quality of provided services and quality control of these services?

Does the Company have the following normative documentation:- International Convention on Standards of Training, Certification and Watch keeping for Seafarers 78/95- Maritime Labor Convention, 2006?- Other national legislative documents in respect of manning services?
Does the Company follow the changes in national and international legislation?

Does the Company have and keep updated the following procedures?- crewing and manning procedure- procedure of keeping personal data of crewmembers applying to the Company- procedure of crewmembers training before the vessel’s embarkation - seafarers’ complaint procedure- procedure for cooperation with ship owner- procedure for organization of communication with crewmembers and actions in emergency situations- procedure of seafarers’ insurance

Several labour supply countries have detailed legislative provisions for their recruitment and placement services licensing regime on their official websites. The company must demonstrate knowledge on the relevant legislation
Is the order of preparation, consideration, approval, issuance and distribution of documentation followed?
Is there restricted access for documentation with respect to personal data confidentiality principles?
Are the following procedures kept in respect of:identification?keeping?definition of keeping terms?withdrawal?
Resources and personnel
Does the General Manager take part in development and implementation of the Company policy?
Does the Company policy comply with the aims of the Company?
Are the Company personnel familiarized with Company policy?
Is the Company policy analyzed periodically?
Is the company (not) operate blacklists?
Interrelations with a ship owner
Has the crewing services agreement been concluded between the Company and the ship owner or his agent?
Has the agreement been prepared in a language mutually understood and in English?

Does the agreement include the following clauses: financial aspect, order of termination and prolongation of the agreement, procedures in case of non-execution of their obligations by contracting parties, arbitration and applicable legislation?
Does the agreement define the obligations and responsibility of the ship owner including the amount of fee paying to the Company?
Has the ship owner provided the copy of collective agreement to the interested parties?
does the company have a list of customers to approach for feedback?
Procedure for development, approval, conclusion of the seafarers’ employment agreement
When concluding the seafarers’ employment agreement, is the standard form in accordance with acting national legislation used?
Is the form of agreement approved by the Company?
Is the agreement composed in English and in a language understood by the crew, if other than English?
Are the seafarers informed about their rights and obligations stated in employment agreements, before being employed or in the process of employment?
Are the relevant conditions given to the seafarers for familiarization with their employment agreements and receiving the copy of employment agreement for consultations?
Procedure for recruitment and checking of knowledge of crew members
Does the Company provide that each vessel is manned with qualified, educated and medically fit seafarers in accordance with international and national requirements?
Are the records about relevant education, preparation, skills and seafarers’ experience kept?
Are the applicants being interviewed, tested and got instructions with the following registration of results?
Does the Company have the procedures providing the relevant familiarization of new personnel and persons receiving new assignments connected with safety and environment prevention pollution?
Have the main instructions for crew members before the vessel’s embarkation been defined and exist in the Company?
Do the crewmembers familiarize with safety management system of the vessel before embarkation?
Procedure for keeping personal records and register of seafarers
Does the Company have procedures on organization of keeping personal records and register of seafarers?
Does this procedure foresee keeping of confidential information received by the Company during preparation of the seafarers’ employment agreement and its non-disclosure to the third persons?
Are the copies of seafarer’s certificates received during conclusion of the contract kept in personal records?
Procedure for organization of connection with crew and actions in emergency situations
Is the register of seafarers being sent to the vessel or applied to the Company kept in paper or electronic format?
Does the company validate certificates held by candidates being offered up?
The company must be capable to handle data responsibly and comply with respective data protection laws such as General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in Europe
Did the Company define the channels of connection and information exchange between crewing manager and ship owner when transferring the incident message or in emergency case?
Does the Company have Emergency plan?

Does the Plan include the following:- Emergency staff and responsibilities of each member?- Procedure of gathering of emergency staff?- Procedure of establishing connection between crewing manager and ship owner?- Procedure for receiving the exact information about the number of crew members onboard?- List of responsible people with phone numbers that must be informed in emergency situations?- Procedure of informing of crew members’ families?- List of full personal data of all persons staying onboard?
Is this plan duly amended?
Are the relevant personnel duly informed about such amendments?
Procedure for seafarers insurance and system of social security of seafarers and members of their families

Does the agreement between the Company and ship owner foresee such conditions of work and social protection of seafarers that correspond to national, international requirements and collective agreements?

Does the Company provide that the ship owner or the Company itself carry out insurance of each seafarer for the following cases:Illness, trauma, injury, death?Unemployment as result of shipwreck?Loss of property?Repatriation?
Does the Company charge seafarers with any fees for employment or other relevant services?
Does the Company have procedure for providing information and consultancy for families of crew members who are onboard?
Does the Company have a contract with insurance companies that are well familiarized with the relevant kinds of insurance and have good reputation?

tables.title: Equipment Table 2
headers: Initial, for the issuance of a Statement of Compliance (SoC)
rows: Renewal, for the renewal of the Statement of Compliance (SoC)
Annual, for the maintenance of the validity of the SoC

tables.title: Equipment Table 4
rows: The undersigned inspector(s) has/have inspected the above-mentioned company and it was found that:
It was demonstrated that the company has adequate procedures in place and comply with the MLC, 2006
the staff of the company vessel is familiar with the requirements of the MLC, 2006 thereof
No SoC to be issued unless any remarks mentioned below to be rectified
A short term SoC MLC (SRPS) valid for 5 months is issued pending the issue of a full term certificate from Head office
The full term SoC MLC (SRPS) is recommended to be issued / renewed / endorsed

items.item: Are the galley appliances operational?
Are appliances using naked flames adequately protected and secured?
Is the galley maintained in clean and efficient conditions?
Are floor drains in the galley, including water/grease traps, in good condition?
Are cold and hot drinking water available at the washing-up area?
Is fresh water available at hose connections for washing down?
In vessel over 1000 GT, is the mechanical ventilation of the galley operational?
Is the artificial lighting of the galley operational?
Are provision store rooms being used exclusively for the intended purpose?
Are provision store rooms being maintained in a clean and efficient condition?
Is the lighting of provision store rooms operational?
Are refrigerated provisions rooms being exclusively used for the intended purpose?
Are the refrigerated spaces maintained in clean and efficient conditions?
Are adequate temperatures maintained inside the refrigerated spaces?
In walk-in rooms, can doors be opened from both sides and is the alarm operational?
Is drinking water available at galleys, main pantries and adjacent to messrooms?
Are drinking and sanitary water tanks being exclusively used for the intended purpose and
intended purpose and kept in an internally clean condition?
Are fresh water evaporators and water treatment equipment, if used to supply drinking or
sanitary fresh water, operational (including automatic control systems and alarms)?
In vessel over 1000 GT, are fresh water power pumps and reserve pumps operational?
In vessel over 1000 GT, is cool drinking water available from al least one source?
Are external bulkheads and other bulkheads required to be watertight or gastight (such as those
separating the crew accommodation from machinery spaces, storerooms, etc) maintained in an
adequate condition?
Is the insulation of external bulkheads and other bulkheads which are required to be insulated
against heat properly maintained?
Is the insulation of steam and hot water pipes crossing the crew accommodation properly
maintained so as to avoid danger and discomfort to the occupants?
If cabins and messroms are painted, is the paint used of a light colour?
Are wall surfaces, floors and ceilings well maintained, and being renewed or restored when necessary?
Are cabins, messrooms, recreations rooms if provided, and public sanitary spaces properly
ventilated, without stale air, odours or an excessive temperature in relation to the outside?
Are the ventilation systems operational?
and to minimize the risk of fire?
Has the inspector verified that portable stoves are not used on board?
Are the sleeping arrangements for the crew in accordance with the ILO / MLC and in particular with the
assignment of numbers and categories of persons to each sleeping room?
Have the furnishings and particulars of each of the sleeping rooms been verified and found acceptable?
In particular, are the ventilation, heating and electric lights of each sleeping room operational?
Are messrooms and their furnishings in good state of maintenance, with numbers of seats, tables,
In particular, are the ventilation, heating and electric lights of each messroom in working order?
Are the open deck areas designated for recreation of the crew in good condition and free of
obstructions which may impede their use?
Is crew recreation accommodation in good condition, adequately furnished, ventilated, heated
and with electric lights ?
Are the public bathrooms in a good state of maintenance, with walls, floors and fixtures in working order?
Are the waste pipes and soil pipes free of obstruction and in working order, without leaks which
may create health hazards?
Are cold fresh water supplies and hot fresh water supplies, or means to heat water, in working
order in all public bathrooms?
Is fresh water available in public bathrooms at all times or if not continuously in adequate
quantities and time periods?
Is flushing water available in WC's at all times and are local controls at each unit operational?
Are private and semi-private bathrooms in good state of maintenance, with fixtures, water supplies
and drains in working order?
Are WC screens, doors and, if fitted, locks in good order?
Are floor drains in bathroom used by more than one person in working order?
Are the facilities for washing clothes details in working order?
Are the facilities for drying clothes also in working order?
If provided, is the hospital in good state of maintenance, not used for other than its intended
purpose and with its bathroom in working order?
Is the medicine chest properly maintained, with its contents valid and renewed as necessary,
and instructions available?
If the ship is of 3000 GT or more, are the deck and engine offices well maintained and used
for the intended purpose?
If the ship regularly trades to mosquito infested areas, are the screens for side scuttles, ventilators
and doors available and in good order?
If the ship regularly trades to the Tropics or the Persian Gulf, and does not have air conditioning,
are the awnings for use in exposed deck area above the crew accommodation and open deck
recreation areas available and in good order?
Are the arrangements on board adapted, as far as is reasonable and practicable, to the particular
national customs of the crew or, when several national groups are employed, are reasonable
arrangements made to cope with these differences?
Is the crew accommodation maintained in a clean and decent condition and free of goods and
stores not the property of the crew?
Are weekly Inspections conducted by the Master, or a person delegated by the Master, accompanied
by one or more members of the crew, and are their results entered in the ship's log book?
